name: Hawaiian Railway Society
tagline: Ride a restored 1900s sugar plantation train along the Ewa coast — the last operating stretch of Oahu's original railway.
parking: Free parking at the depot on Renton Rd
hours: Train rides Sundays at 1pm and 3pm; special events monthly
bestTime: The 3pm ride catches golden light along the coast; check for holiday-themed runs
talkStory: Founded in 1971 by train enthusiasts who salvaged equipment from the defunct Oahu Railway & Land Company, which Benjamin Dillingham built in 1889 to haul sugar from Ewa plantations. The all-volunteer society operates restored 1900s rolling stock on the last 6.5 miles of track along the Ewa coast. One of their steam engines dates to 1890. It's the only operating heritage railway in Hawaii and a National Register historic site.
